commands.review_summary:
The PR removes Codex app-server compaction timeout restart/retry, blocks queued Codex harness fallback into context-engine compaction, updates regression tests, and rewrites the changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main's CLI compaction test still expects stale Codex native bindings to fall back into context-engine compaction. I did not run the tests because this review is read-only and the checkout must remain byte-for-byte clean.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer/contributor handling because the PR has a protected label, lacks real behavior proof, and has a remaining CLI boundary gap that should be fixed or explicitly accepted before merge.

target.title: Fail Codex compaction at the Codex boundary
target.body:
## Summary

Codex runtime compaction had the wrong failure behavior. When native Codex app-server compaction timed out, OpenClaw restarted the shared app-server and retried. That killed unrelated live turns using the same app-server, and the retry could not find the old thread because Codex `thread/compact/start` only operates on threads currently loaded in that app-server process.

After that, the embedded runner treated the failed harness compaction as recoverable and fell through to OpenClaw context-engine compaction. For Codex-backed OpenAI sessions that can drift into public OpenAI Responses summarization with Codex OAuth/session metadata, which is exactly the bad class of failure we do not want.

This changes the contract: Codex runtime sessions compact through native Codex, or they fail honestly. We no longer restart the shared app-server as a compaction retry strategy, and Codex harness compaction failures no longer fall back to OpenClaw/public OpenAI compaction. Stale `thread not found` bindings are still cleared, but the current compaction fails instead of silently switching runtimes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex-runtime compaction timeout should not kill the shared Codex app-server, retry against a fresh app-server, or fall back into OpenClaw/public OpenAI compaction.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout using the Codex app-server compaction seam and embedded runner compaction seam.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/compact.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`; `pnpm check:changed`; `pnpm build`.

Evidence after fix: The Codex app-server compaction timeout regression test now asserts a single `thread/compact/start` request, zero `client.close()` calls, the binding remains intact, and the result fails with the native timeout reason. The embedded runner regression test now asserts Codex native binding failures return the harness failure directly and do not call `contextEngine.compact`.

Observed result after fix: Targeted tests, changed checks, and build all passed locally.

What was not tested: I did not force a destructive live compaction timeout on Pash's running dev agent session.
target.author: pashpashpash
